lightBloom.note: Large phosphor digits should bleed light into surrounding dark panel
Each GO lamp needs a soft halo — they are the jewels of this scene
LEDs should bloom on each blink peak then decay

lensFlare.note: Persistent subtle horizontal streak across the MET clock — CRT refraction artifact

chromaticAberration.note: Subtle RGB fringing toward screen edges — old CRT phosphor misalignment

filmGrain.note: Good as specified, but should be monochrome green-tinted, not neutral gray

scanLines.note: Present but need horizontal jitter — every ~120 frames, scan lines should shift 1px vertically for 3 frames to simulate sync wobble

crtBarrelDistortion.note: 0.012 is good. Add subtle horizontal bowing — left/right edges should curve inward 2px

reflectionGlare.note: CRT glass reflection — faint diagonal white gradient from top-left corner, suggests overhead fluorescent lighting

dustMotes.note: MOCR was dusty. Faint particles drifting in the foreground, out-of-focus bokeh circles

particleEffects.note: Occasional single-pixel bright flash on random screen locations — old CRT phosphor pop

cameraDrift.note: Slight handheld drift suggesting a documentary cameraman in the MOCR

rackFocus.note: At frame 80, subtly shift focus from center plot to the CAPCOM log as a new critical entry arrives

dramaticReveals.note: Banner text should type out character by character on composition start

breathingAnimations.note: Central node breathes — it is alive
Panels very subtly brighten/dim — simulates CRT power supply ripple

microInteractions.note: Needle should have high-frequency micro-vibration on top of its oscillation — mechanical instrument feel

transitionMoments.description: Agent-gamma CAUTION event — go-nogo board flashes, capcom log gets amber entry, waveform shows spike, ambient light subtly shifts amber for 2 seconds

dataDensityGaps.region: centerBottom gap between trajectory-data and flight-director-status
dataDensityGaps.fix: Add a thin horizontal rule with 'FLIGHT DYNAMICS OFFICER' label centered, plus small auxiliary readouts: ORBIT NUMBER, GROUND ELAPSED TIME
Space is tight but add a small 'REVISION: 2026.068 | BUILD: c9op-4.2.1' watermark
Add columns: TOKENS USED, ERROR COUNT, LAST ERROR
Add a faint 'CONFIDENTIAL — INTERNAL USE ONLY' watermark diagonally across the background
Add a 50x50px grid overlay at 0.04 opacity across the entire background — graph paper feel

colorGradingNotes.overallLook: Cold green phosphor with warm amber accents — like shooting 16mm Kodak 7218 through a green-tinted CRT. Shadows should have slight blue-black tint (#0a0f14), not pure black

keyMoments.shift: Ambient background subtly warms to #0f0f0a for 2 seconds — amber contamination from the warning

colorGradingNotes.accentUsage: Amber (#ffaa33) is underused. The mission-phase-label is amber but nothing else uses it. Add amber accents to the strip-chart threshold lines and table header borders for visual rhythm
colorGradingNotes.backgroundAtmosphere: Background should not be flat #0a0f0a. Use a very subtle radial gradient: center #0d130d, edges #060a06 — suggests the CRT is brighter in the center

typographyNotes.fonts: IBM Plex Mono is perfect for this — it has the right NASA/IBM heritage
typographyNotes.monospaceReadout: IBM Plex Mono at weight 600 is correct for data. Consider adding tabular-nums font-feature-settings for aligned columns
typographyNotes.displayFont: Title could use Space Mono for slight visual distinction from data text
typographyNotes.sizeHierarchy: The clock at 36px dominates correctly. But the 10px labels are too small for readability at 1080p — bump to 11px minimum. The 14px readout size is good but needs more leading (1.6x)

continuousAmbience.sound: Rhythmic soft beep on each LED blink — the heartbeat of the room
Low-frequency white noise hum proportional to waveform amplitude

eventSounds.sound: Soft teletype click-clack as each character appears

silenceMoments.description: Brief pause in all animation just before the agent-gamma caution event — the room holds its breath

soundDesignImplied.visualRhythm: The scrolling capcom log should have entries arrive in bursts (3 entries close together, then silence) rather than metronomic spacing — real comms are bursty
